# Computed GP guard-filter route-attribution telemetry — the live record-seam (AC1+AC2)

## Context

Narrow landed-pieces close-target for PR #15060, split from #15057 per @neo-gpt's cycle-1 review RA-1: the PR must not claim to feed the #14503 type-gate disposition when it records **guard-filter** telemetry, not type-gate rejections. This leaf scopes the truthfully-delivered record-seam; **#15057 stays open** for AC3.

## What this leaf covers (delivered in PR #15060)

The live per-synthesis-run record-seam for the **routing contradiction guard**: which computed content/narrative candidates the guard filtered, under which **arming reasons** (only the `CURRENT_FOCUS_ROUTING_CONFLICT_REASONS` that actually armed the guard — incident/prio-zero — attributed via the shared `getRoutingConflictReasons` authority; incidental co-reasons kept separately as `candidateReasons`). No exclusion-label dimension: every guard-blocked node already passed the actionability type-gate, so its exclusion set is empty by construction — that evidence belongs to the type-gate producer (AC3), not this guard-filter one.

- **AC1** — a live JSONL record-seam exists (writes per synthesis run from the orchestrator daemon, `routeAttributionLedgerStore`; never a git-tracked `measurements/*.md`).
- **AC2** — guard-filtered candidates recorded with correct arming-reason attribution; `summarize`/`query` fold `byArmingReason` / `blockedNodeCounts`; fail-open emit at the synthesis boundary (a write failure never alters synthesis output), hermetically test-isolated under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` (no prod `.neo-ai-data` write).

## Out of Scope (stays on #15057 as AC3)

- The **#14503 42.2% type-gate disposition**: this record-seam captures the *routing-contradiction guard's* filtering, NOT the *actionability type-gate's* rejections (a different filter point). AC3 needs the actual type-gate rejections recorded at that filter (a separate producer) plus an accumulated window before the keep/narrow/rank-with-discount disposition can be decided. #15057 remains the open contract for it.

## Related

- Parent: #15057 (record-seam ticket — stays open for AC3) · #14503 (type-gate disposition) · #14609 / #14472 (GP-v2) · PR #15060 (delivers this leaf).

- 2026-07-12T05:19:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `539c900` - "feat(graph): computed-GP guard-filter route-attribution telemetry — record-seam (#15068) (#15060)

* feat(graph): route-attribution ledger store — the live #14454 record-seam primitive (#15057)

The #14454 route-attribution ledger had no live writer: its only artifact was a
one-shot committed measurement dataset. This adds the durable append-only JSONL
store that the computed-GP contradiction guard's blocked-candidate stream writes
to per synthesis run — mirroring the healEventLedgerStore primitive (pure edge
I/O, injected clock, no config-SSOT read, no helper-owned retention default;
fail-open reads, self-bounding prune only when the AiConfig boundary supplies the
policy). summarize/query fold the stream into the evidence surface the #14503
type-gate disposition reads (blocked-node counts, by focus reason, by exclusion
bucket). GoldenPathSynthesizer runs in the orchestrator daemon (runtime), so this
is a JSONL store in a runtime state dir, never a git-tracked measurements/*.md.

Store primitive + 12 hermetic specs; the fail-open synthesis emit lands next.

* feat(graph): wire the route-attribution record-seam emit — fail-open, both guard branches (#15057)

AC2 of the record-seam. GoldenPathSynthesizer now records which computed candidates
the routing contradiction guard filtered (BOTH the partial-block and no-survivor
branches) into the route-attribution ledger, per synthesis run:

- buildRouteAttributionRecords (pure): blockedNodes -> records tagged with the
  focus-reason union across the armed Current Focus candidates + the per-node
  exclusion bucket.
- recordRouteAttribution (fail-open boundary): reads the retention/dir leaves at
  the use site (read-at-use-site, no defensive optional-chaining, no threading),
  validates, passes them EXPLICITLY into the pure store helper; a write failure is
  swallow-logged, never aborts synthesis.
- New AiConfig leaves in the memory-core goldenPath* set: LedgerDir/MaxEvents/
  PruneTriggerBytes; config.mjs re-materialized (overlay, gitignored).

3 mapping/fail-safe specs green; 59 existing synthesizer specs unbroken; 12 store
specs green. Makes the seam non-speculative; the disposition-feed follows a window.

* fix(graph): restore destructuring block-align --fix mangled into a syntax error (#15057)

check-block-alignment --fix corrupted
  const {blockedNodes = [], focusCandidates = []} = focusContradiction, focusReasons = ...
— it choked on the = defaults INSIDE the destructuring pattern, ate '[], focusCandidates = []} ='
and produced 'const {blockedNodes = focusContradiction, ...' (SyntaxError 387:139). It shipped
because --fix runs in lint-staged AFTER the local test pass, so the aligned version was never
run locally. Rewritten as block-align-stable plain consts (Array.isArray guards replace the
destructuring defaults). Verified: node --check + 15 specs pass on the post-hook file.

* fix(test): restore Synthesizer binding a replace_all clobbered from the main describe (#15057)

The AC2 commit removed 3 redundant per-test 'const Synthesizer = GoldenPathSynthesizer.constructor'
lines via replace_all — but that exact form also existed in the main describe's EXISTING tests, so
it deleted their bindings too. GoldenPathSynthesizer.spec.mjs:163 (the first user) threw
ReferenceError; mode:'serial' then SKIPPED the rest, surfacing in CI as a lone '1 failed' that masked
the cascade. Fixed by binding Synthesizer once in the main describe's beforeAll (mirrors the
hasCrossFamilyReview describe). Full spec: 50 passed / 0 failed / 0 skipped.

* fix(graph): attribute only guard causes — armingReasons vs candidateReasons (#15057)

Cycle-2 RA-2 (@neo-gpt review on #15060). buildRouteAttributionRecords recorded the
UNION of all focus-candidate reasons — mis-attributing incidental co-reasons
(fresh-updated, agent-os) as guard triggers. Now it derives armingReasons from the SAME
getRoutingConflictReasons authority isRoutingConflictFocusCandidate predicates on
(incident/prio-zero), keeping the full diagnostic set separately as candidateReasons.
The store folds byArmingReason and queries by armingReasons. Exact incidental-reason
case: {incident, fresh-updated} -> armingReasons [incident], candidateReasons both. 15 passed.

* test(graph): hermetic route-attribution emit via injected ledger dir (#15057)

Cycle-2 RA-3 (@neo-gpt review on #15060). recordRouteAttribution now takes the ledger
dir + retention as params (the synthesis boundary reads the resolved AiConfig leaves at
its use site and passes them), making the real emit a testable seam without an aiConfig
read of its own — validation + fail-open stay inside the method. New synthesis specs pin
it hermetically with a per-test temp dir: non-empty writes for partial-block + no-survivor
(with correct armingReasons attribution), a real write failure (mkdir under a file) does
not throw so synthesis is unaffected, and nothing touches the production .neo-ai-data
default (records provably land in the injected dir). 5 passed.

* fix(graph): cycle-3 — drop phantom exclusion-label dimension + test-isolate synthesis ledger (#15057)

Cycle-2 RC (@neo-gpt on #15060).

RA-1 (attribute only populated dimensions): every guard-blocked node ALREADY passed the
actionability type-gate (predicate: getComputedRecommendationExclusionLabels().length === 0),
so its exclusion set is empty by construction — exclusionLabels was a phantom dimension (exact
runtime row: exclusionLabels []). Removed it from the record + byExclusionLabel from summarize +
the exclusionLabels query filter; the guard-filter producer now describes only what it populates
(armingReasons, blockedNodeId, candidateReasons). The rejection-bucket belongs to the type-gate producer.

RA-3 (hermetic synthesis emit): the direct recordRouteAttribution seam tests used temp dirs but
did not drive synthesizeGoldenPath, so the synthesis specs still wrote the prod .neo-ai-data ledger.
Fixed by construction: goldenPathRouteAttributionLedgerDir is now a Prod/Test formula gated on
UNIT_TEST_MODE (mirrors storagePaths.graph / handoffFilePath), so synthesis-triggered emits resolve
to an OS-temp dir under test. Proof test asserts the resolved dir is the test path, not .neo-ai-data.

Rebased onto dev (merged #15058 touched the same synthesizer/routing/spec surfaces). 18 specs green.
